paper_title: GenEdit: Compounding Operators and Continuous Improvement to Tackle Text-to-SQL in the Enterprise
main_problem: Deploying Text-to-SQL solutions in enterprise settings faces challenges including understanding external knowledge (company-specific terminology), handling large query complexity, and systematic improvement over time
proposed_solution: GenEdit system with three main components: 1) Knowledge set containing natural language-SQL pairs, instructions, and schemas; 2) Multi-operator pipeline using LLM calls to decompose problems; 3) Continuous improvement through user feedback
key_features: Interactive copilot for user feedback iteration
Staged edits that update generation prompts
Feedback merging after regression testing and approval
Edits recommendation module for subject matter experts

technical_approach.architecture: Multi-operator pipeline to reduce LLM reasoning complexity

example_query.description: Identify sports organizations with best/worst quarter-over-quarter financial performance in Canada for Q2 2023

related_work.benchmark_limitations: Current popular public benchmarks don't match enterprise query complexity

title: GenEdit: Compounding Operators and Continuous Improvement to Tackle Text-to-SQL in the Enterprise
